boundary_notes: Edge cases vs other skills: pure token shape with no isolation → core; visualization rendering → data-visualization; editor that exposes filters → also analytics-studio (editors can bypass weak patterns); per-tenant database setup → also data-integration; suspected breach → troubleshooting FIRST to confirm.

evals.prompt: We have a multi-tenant SaaS app where all customers share the same Luzmo dataset but each row has a tenant_id column. When embedding dashboards we need each customer to only see their own rows. What's the recommended way to enforce this?
evals.expected_output: Recommend Pattern 1 (dataset-level EmbedFilterGroup with parameter_overrides). The EmbedFilterGroup enforces the filter at the dataset level so even editor users cannot bypass it. Guide through the three steps: create EmbedFilterGroup, associate it to the dataset, pass parameter_overrides in createAuthorization.
expectations: Recommends `EmbedFilterGroup` with parameter filtering as the primary approach
Explains the three steps: create EmbedFilterGroup, associate to dataset, pass `parameter_overrides` in `createAuthorization`
States that dataset-level filtering cannot be bypassed even by editor users
References `https://developer.luzmo.com/api/createEmbedFilterGroup.md`
References `https://developer.luzmo.com/api/associateEmbedFilterGroup.md`
References `https://developer.luzmo.com/api/createAuthorization.md`

evals.prompt: Each of our enterprise customers has their own separate PostgreSQL database with identical schema. How do I configure Luzmo so that embedding a dashboard for Customer A queries Customer A's DB, and Customer B gets Customer B's DB?
evals.expected_output: Direct the user to Pattern 3 (account_overrides). The createAuthorization call should include account_overrides with the per-tenant connection details. Should fetch the connection-overrides docs before describing the exact payload shape.
expectations: Identifies `account_overrides` as the correct mechanism
Explains that `account_overrides` is set inside the `createAuthorization` request server-side
References `https://developer.luzmo.com/guide/dashboard-embedding--handling-multi-tenant-data--connection-overrides.md`
References `https://developer.luzmo.com/api/createAuthorization.md`
Does not suggest mixing up tenant data by reusing one connection for all tenants

evals.prompt: We have an EmbedFilterGroup set up to filter by tenant_id, but one of our internal admin users needs to see all tenant data in the embedded dashboard — no filter applied. How do we clear the parameter for that user?
evals.expected_output: Explain that parameter_overrides supports a clear flag: { 'clear': true } which removes the filter constraint for that embed token. This is documented in the createAuthorization API.
expectations: States that `parameter_overrides` with `{ "clear": true }` removes the filter for that token
References `https://developer.luzmo.com/api/createAuthorization.md`
Does not suggest deleting or modifying the EmbedFilterGroup itself

evals.prompt: When should I use token-level filters (the 'filters' field in createAuthorization) versus setting up a dataset-level EmbedFilterGroup? I'm not sure which is more secure.
evals.expected_output: A clear comparison: dataset-level EmbedFilterGroup (Pattern 1) is stronger because it prevents even editor users from bypassing the filter. Token-level filters are enforced only at query time and an editor with sufficient rights could potentially work around them. Recommend EmbedFilterGroup when end-users have edit access.
expectations: States that dataset-level `EmbedFilterGroup` is stronger — editors cannot bypass it
States that dashboard-level or token-level filters are weaker — editors can potentially remove them
Recommends `EmbedFilterGroup` for editor scenarios
References `https://developer.luzmo.com/guide/dashboard-embedding--handling-multi-tenant-data.md`

evals.prompt: Can I just filter the data in my React frontend after I fetch it from Luzmo? That way I can show each user only their data without touching the embed token.
evals.expected_output: Firmly reject client-side isolation as insecure: all multi-tenant filtering must be in the server-side createAuthorization call. Client-side filtering means all tenant data is fetched and exposed in the browser.
expectations: Clearly states that client-side filtering is insecure for data isolation
Explains that without server-side filtering, all tenant data is sent to the browser
States that all multi-tenant logic must be in the server-side `createAuthorization` call
Does not present client-side filtering as a valid alternative

evals.prompt: Can you walk me through all the fields I can set in the createAuthorization API request body? I want to understand what role, access, expiry, and username mean.
evals.expected_output: This is a general token generation and auth API question — multitenancy skill should recognize it goes beyond multi-tenant filtering and route to core for the full createAuthorization property reference.
expectations: Recognizes this is a general auth/token question, not specifically a multi-tenant isolation question
Routes to `core` for the full `createAuthorization` property reference
Does NOT attempt to fully document all createAuthorization fields from memory
References `https://developer.luzmo.com/api/createAuthorization.md` for the canonical field list

evals.prompt: We run a reseller platform. Each reseller partner manages their own end-customers, and end-customers should only see their own data rows. So we need two levels: partners see only their customers' data, and end-customers see only their own rows. Can Luzmo handle two levels of tenant hierarchy, and what's the right setup?
evals.expected_output: Explanation that Luzmo supports two-level hierarchy by combining suborganization (for partner-level isolation of the user pool) with EmbedFilterGroup + parameter_overrides (for end-customer row-level isolation). The embed token uses suborganization to scope the partner context and parameter_overrides to filter to the specific end-customer's rows within that partition. Should clarify that suborganization alone does not provide row-level data isolation.
expectations: Explains that two-level hierarchy requires combining `suborganization` with `EmbedFilterGroup` + `parameter_overrides`
States that `suborganization` handles partner-level isolation (each partner's user pool is scoped separately)
States that `parameter_overrides` + `EmbedFilterGroup` handles row-level isolation for end-customers within each partner
References `https://developer.luzmo.com/api/createAuthorization.md` for both `suborganization` and `parameter_overrides`
Does NOT claim that `suborganization` alone provides row-level data isolation
